skills.name: codex-cli
skills.desc: Lightweight coding agent that runs in your terminal

skills.name: define-goal
skills.desc: Help the user define a concrete, measurable goal before starting work, especially when they ask to use the goal tool, create a goal, set an objective, clarify success criteria, or turn a fuzzy intention into a quantitative outcome. Use this

skills.name: gh-address-comments
skills.desc: Help address review/issue comments on the open GitHub PR for the current branch using gh CLI; verify gh auth first and prompt the user to authenticate if not logged in.

skills.name: migrate-to-codex
skills.desc: Migrate supported instruction files, skills, agents, and MCP config into Codex project and global files.

skills.name: netlify-deploy
skills.desc: Deploy web projects to Netlify using the Netlify CLI (`npx netlify`). Use when the user asks to deploy, host, publish, or link a site/repo on Netlify, including preview and production deploys.

skills.name: screenshot
skills.desc: "Use when the user explicitly asks for a desktop or system screenshot (full screen, specific app or window, or a pixel region), or when tool-specific capture capabilities are unavailable and an OS-level capture is needed."

skills.name: sentry
skills.desc: "Use when the user asks to inspect Sentry issues or events, summarize recent production errors, or pull basic Sentry health data via the Sentry CLI; perform read-only queries using the `sentry` command."

skills.name: yeet
skills.desc: "Use only when the user explicitly asks to stage, commit, push, and open a GitHub pull request in one flow using the GitHub CLI (`gh`)."
